# qbuem-auth

C++23 authentication library built on qbuem-stack.
HS256 JWT + async OpenSSL HTTPS client + OAuth2 for Google / GitHub / Discord / Microsoft / Facebook / Naver / Kakao.

## File Structure

- `src/auth/jwt.hpp`          — HS256 JWT (qbuem::crypto only, no OpenSSL)
- `src/auth/https_client.hpp` — HTTPS client (OpenSSL BIO + eventfd Reactor integration)
- `src/auth/oauth.hpp`        — OAuth2 providers with common UserInfo struct
- `CMakeLists.txt`            — qbuem-stack FetchContent + OpenSSL find_package

## CMake Target

- `qbuem-auth::auth` — INTERFACE library (header-only)

## JWT API

```cpp
// Claims: sub(int64), provider, email, name, iat, exp, type(TokenType)
// TokenType: TokenType::Access | TokenType::Refresh

std::string access_token  = jwt::encode(claims);          // access token (24h)
std::string refresh_token = jwt::encode_refresh(claims);  // refresh token (30d)

std::optional<jwt::Claims> c = jwt::decode(token);                           // verify access
std::optional<jwt::Claims> c = jwt::decode(token, jwt::TokenType::Refresh);  // verify refresh
std::optional<std::string_view> t = jwt::extract_bearer(auth_header);
```

Implementation: `base64url(header).base64url(payload).base64url(HMAC-SHA256(h.p, secret))`
- Secret: `JWT_SECRET` env var or CSPRNG 32-byte auto-generated
- Signature verification: `qbuem::crypto::constant_time_equal()` (timing-safe)
- Payload includes `"type":"access"|"refresh"` — type mismatch rejected on `decode()`
- TTL: AccessToken=24h, RefreshToken=30d

## HTTPS Client

High-performance design:
- `ThreadPool::global()` — `hardware_concurrency()` worker pool. No OS thread creation per request.
- `ConnPool::global()` — per-host TLS connection reuse (max 4 per host, 25s idle timeout).
- `shared_ssl_ctx()` — `SSL_CTX` created once per process.
- Zero-copy body — erase-front + move, no separate `resp_body` allocation.
- `EventFdAwaiter` — non-blocking on Reactor thread.

```cpp
auto resp = co_await https::post(url, body);
auto resp = co_await https::post(url, body, content_type, extra_headers);
auto resp = co_await https::get(url);
auto resp = co_await https::get(url, extra_headers);
```

Response struct:
```cpp
struct Response {
    int         status = 0;
    std::string body;
    std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string> headers;  // keys lowercased

    bool ok() const noexcept;
    std::string_view header(std::string_view key) const noexcept;  // case-insensitive
};
```

`extra_headers`: `"Header-Name: value\r\n"` format string.

`EventFdAwaiter`: `await_suspend()` calls `Reactor::register_event(efd, Read, resume_handler)`.
TLS: `SSL_VERIFY_PEER` + system CA + SNI + HTTP/1.1 `Connection: keep-alive`.

## OAuth2

`UserInfo`: `{ provider, provider_id, email, name, avatar_url }`

CSRF state store (declared before providers):
- `state_store::issue()` — mutex-protected, auto-expires stale entries, TTL 10 min
- `state_store::verify_and_consume(state)` — one-shot, thread-safe

All provider `exchange()` functions perform CSRF verification via the `state` parameter:
```cpp
Provider::exchange(code, state)  // returns nullopt on CSRF failure
```

Provider-specific notes:
- **GoogleProvider**: OIDC scope=openid+email+profile, userinfo via Authorization Bearer
- **NaverProvider**: `state` included in token request (Naver spec), response unwrapped via `json_get_obj("response")`
- **KakaoProvider**: `id` is integer, `kakao_account.profile` nested, returns nullopt if `kakao_account` missing
- **GitHubProvider**: `User-Agent` required, `Accept:application/json` on token request, falls back to `/user/emails` API for private emails
- **DiscordProvider**: avatar URL assembled from `id+hash`, `global_name` → `username` fallback
- **MicrosoftProvider**: tenant-specific URL (`MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID`, default `common`), email from `mail` then `userPrincipalName`, avatar requires Bearer auth
- **FacebookProvider**: token exchange via POST (client_secret in body), `picture.data.url` nested

Data consistency:
- All providers build `UserInfo::provider` from `kName` constant (not hard-coded strings)
- `authorize_url()` state parameter goes through `qbuem::url_encode()`

JSON parsing helpers:
- `json_get_field_view()` — zero-copy fast-path for unescaped string fields
- `json_get_field()` — uses `_view` fast-path internally; handles escaped/scalar/null
- `json_get_obj()` — returns `string_view` into `"key":{...}` content (zero-copy)

CSRF state_store: 16-shard FNV-1a hash. Eliminates global mutex contention.

Environment variables: `{GOOGLE,NAVER,KAKAO,GITHUB,DISCORD,MICROSOFT,FACEBOOK}_{CLIENT_ID,CLIENT_SECRET}`,
`MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID` (default `common`), `OAUTH_REDIRECT_BASE`.

## Dependencies

- **qbuem-stack**: `qbuem::crypto` (HMAC-SHA256, Base64url, CSPRNG, constant_time_equal),
  `qbuem::url_encode/decode`, `qbuem::Reactor` (eventfd integration), `qbuem::Task<T>`
- **OpenSSL**: SSL/TLS for HTTPS outbound only
